mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.16% in Dec 2005 reflects recent tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 357.8B in Dec 2005 reflects strong consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods produce negative average returns in > 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.2% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% in Dec 2005 is near cycle lows
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp fail to precede negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the signal is rapidly incorporated.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 108.96 in Nov 2005 reflects manufacturing activity
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration events fail to produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline while simultaneously inflation accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, indicating stagflationary pressure where demand destruction in housing is insufficient to offset price pressures, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2123K in Nov 2005 reflects housing boom peak
fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.8 in Nov 2005 shows moderate inflation
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with housing demand destruction
failure_condition: Joint HOUST decline and CPIAUCSL acceleration fail to precede negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200K month-over-month while simultaneously FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 4.0%, indicating the Fed maintains restrictive policy despite strong labor demand, creating tension that resolves in equity market weakness over 60 trading days as growth expectations are eventually revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134.5M in Dec 2005 reflects strong employment
fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.16% in Dec 2005 reflects restrictive stance
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with strong labor market
failure_condition: Joint strong payroll growth and elevated FEDFUNDS fail to precede negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerates above 3.5% annualized while housing starts simultaneously decline, indicating demand is shifting away from residential investment toward other sectors, creating portfolio rotation that produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as non-housing equities benefit from growth acceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3.5% AND HOUST month-over-month change < -2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11202.3B in Q3 2005 reflects strong growth
fact:HOUST at 2123K in Nov 2005 reflects housing market peak
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand rotation away from housing
failure_condition: Joint GDP acceleration and housing decline fail to precede positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation-adjusted real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth) exceeds 2.0%, indicating restrictive monetary policy in real terms that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the cumulative effect of tight policy materializes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.0 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.8 in Nov 2005 reflects moderate inflation environment
failure_condition: High real federal funds rate fails to precede negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of real rate construct < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
